narration: Explain the concept of merge sort by describing how to merge two sorted arrays into one sorted array.
Explain the divide and conquer approach to sorting an unsorted array by recursively dividing it into smaller sorted subarrays and merging them.
Implement a function to merge two sorted arrays by comparing elements and appending the smaller one to a result list.
Fix the merge function to handle remaining elements after one array is exhausted by appending them directly.
Implement the merge sort algorithm using recursion to divide the array and merge sorted subarrays.
Refactor the merge sort implementation to sort in-place without creating new arrays to optimize space complexity.
Test the merge sort implementation with various edge cases including empty lists, single elements, reverse sorted, and random lists.
Implement a modified merge sort function that takes a key and a descending flag to sort JSON objects based on specific attributes.
